chunks.text: On the earth, there are more than 6,000 known species of mammal alone.
The number of insect species is estimated in the millions.
Tropical rainforests cover only about six percent of the earth's surface
yet more than half of all species dwell there.
These " hotspots of biodiversity " are shrinking rapidly.
Logging, conversion to farmland, urban development.
Human activity is steadily taking the habitat of animals away.
Plastic pollution torments whale and turtle
and the chemicals carried by the tide threaten the food chain.
But conservation efforts are beginning to yield results.
A certain tribe has protected the forest of its ancestors and established a pioneer model of community - led conservation.
An ecosystem is built on a subtle balance.
The loss of one species affects others in a chain.
Whether we can find the wisdom to coexist with nature will determine the future of this planet.
